mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, the discount rate on equities declines and liquidity conditions ease, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if declining FEDFUNDS periods do not produce higher 20-day forward SPX returns than non-declining periods, or if the sign is negative in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rise-triggered windows do not show lower 20-day SPX returns than unconditional windows, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates stronger retail demand and payroll-supported spending, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-PAYEMS improvement does not outperform either proxy alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term has no incremental sign-consistent effect.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates while HOUST also rises, it signals a broad cyclical production rebound that should support equity returns over the next quarter via stronger earnings and industrial activity.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with simultaneous INDPRO and HOUST strength do not produce higher 60-day SPX returns than periods with only one improving, or if the interaction effect is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising FEDFUNDS rate suppresses valuation multiples and increases financial tightening, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week once the policy rate is clearly restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 5-day forward returns are not negative after FEDFUNDS crosses 4.0%, or if triggered windows show positive returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDPC1 with stable or falling UNRATE signals resilient aggregate demand and labor income, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if growth episodes in GDPC1 paired with non-rising UNRATE do not outperform other periods on 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction sign is unstable across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS contracts after a prior expansion, it marks a labor-market inflection that should lead equity prices lower over the next month as consumers and corporate demand weaken.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month PAYEMS declines are not followed by lower 20-day SPX returns than the unconditional average, or if the sign is positive in most triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a mild inflation environment, a faster rise in CPIAUCSL alongside steady retail sales signals pricing power without severe policy tightening, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises year-over-year while FEDFUNDS stays below 3.0%
failure_condition: Reject if low-rate inflation episodes with stable retail sales do not deliver positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term becomes negative when FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%.
